description: A small span from one side to the other is 'hieru'. A narrow doorway may lead into a large room, and a narrow plank may still be 'theru' (thick). 'lonai' (width) names the dimension being judged.
articulatory_notes: Let unvoiced air pass through 'h' into high-front unrounded 'i'. Do not glide into the next vowel; begin a separate front 'e' and stress it. A tap or short trill starts the final syllable before rounded high-back 'u'.
usage_notes: The adjective commonly describes passages and openings, as in 'hieru ruela' (narrow path). Figuratively, it can limit the breadth of an inquiry or focus.

semantic_domains.physical: restricted width in passages and openings
semantic_domains.spatial: limited span from one side to the other
